number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n183\t//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n184\t//   - expected: What was expected (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n185\t//   - actual: What was actually found (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n186\t//\n187\t// Returns a properly initialized ParseError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n188\t//\n189\t// Example usage:\n190\t//\n191\t//\terr := NewParseError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid syntax\", 10, 5, ErrCodeInvalidSyntax, \"identifier\", \"123\")\n192\tfunc NewParseError(filePath string, message string, line int, column int, code ErrorCode, expected string, actual string) *ParseError {\n193\t\t// Use provided code or default to generic parse error\n194\t\terrorCode := code\n195\t\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n196\t\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeParseError\n197\t\t}\n198\t\n199\t\treturn &ParseError{\n200\t\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n201\t\t\tMessage:   message,\n202\t\t\tLine:      line,\n203\t\t\tColumn:    column,\n204\t\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n205\t\t\tErrorType: ErrorTypeParse,\n206\t\t\tExpected:  expected,\n207\t\t\tActual:    actual,\n208\t\t}\n209\t}\n210\t\n211\t// IsParseError checks if an error is a ParseError.\n212\tfunc IsParseError(err error) bool {\n213\t\tvar pe *ParseError\n214\t\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &pe) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeParse))\n215\t}\n216\t\n217\t// ValidationError represents errors that occur during YAML validation.\n218\t//\n219\t// ValidationError wraps validation failures and provides detailed\n220\t// information about what failed validation. This is the base error type\n221\t// used throughout the yamlutil package for validation errors.\n222\ttype ValidationError struct {\n223\t\tFilePath   string    // Path to the file being validated\n224\t\tFieldPath  string    // Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n225\t\tMessage    string    // Human-readable error message\n226\t\tLine       int       // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n227\t\tColumn     int       // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, optional)\n228\t\tConstraint string    // Constraint that was violated (optional)\n229\t\tContextStr string    // Additional context about the validation state (optional)\n230\t\tErr        error     // Underlying error for error wrapping (optional)\n231\t\tErrorCode  ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling (optional)\n232\t\tType       ErrorType // Category of error for type switching\n233\t}\n234\t\n235\t// Code implements YAMLError interface.\n236\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Code() ErrorCode {\n237\t\tif ve.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n238\t\t\treturn ve.ErrorCode\n239\t\t}\n240\t\treturn ErrCodeValidationFailed\n241\t}\n242\t\n243\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n244\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n245\t\t// If Type is explicitly set, use it\n246\t\tif ve.Type != \"\" {\n247\t\t\treturn ve.Type\n248\t\t}\n249\t\t// Try to determine error type from error code or message\n250\t\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeRequiredField || (ve.FieldPath != \"\" && containsRequiredFieldKeywords(ve.Message)) {\n251\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeFieldNotFound\n252\t\t}\n253\t\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeConstraintViolation || containsConstraintKeywords(ve.Message) {\n254\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeConstraint\n255\t\t}\n256\t\treturn ErrorTypeValidation\n257\t}\n258\t\n259\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n260\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Context() string {\n261\t\treturn ve.ContextStr\n262\t}\n263\t\n264\t// Error implements the error interface.\n265\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Error() string {\n266\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n267\t\n268\t\t// Build base error with location\n269\t\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n270\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s at line %d\", ve.FilePath, ve.Line))\n271\t\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n272\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", ve.Column))\n273\t\t\t}\n274\t\t} else {\n275\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s\", ve.FilePath))\n276\t\t}\n277\t\n278\t\t// Add field path if available\n279\t\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n280\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" at field %s\", ve.FieldPath))\n281\t\t}\n282\t\n283\t\t// Add message\n284\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", ve.Message))\n285\t\n286\t\t// Add constraint if available\n287\t\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n288\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (constraint: %s)\", ve.Constraint))\n289\t\t}\n290\t\n291\t\treturn sb.String()\n292\t}\n293\t\n294\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\n295\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Unwrap() error {\n296\t\treturn ve.Err\n297\t}\n298\t\n299\t// String returns a formatted error message with context.\n300\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) String() string {\n301\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n302\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", ve.Message))\n303\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Type: %s\\n\", ve.YAMLErrorType()))\n304\t\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n305\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Location: Line %d\", ve.Line))\n306\t\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n307\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Column %d\", ve.Column))\n308\t\t\t}\n309\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n310\t\t}\n311\t\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n312\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Field: %s\\n\", ve.FieldPath))\n313\t\t}\n314\t\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n315\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Constraint: %s\\n\", ve.Constraint))\n316\t\t}\n317\t\tif ve.ContextStr != \"\" {\n318\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context: %s\\n\", ve.ContextStr))\n319\t\t}\n320\t\treturn sb.String()\n321\t}\n322\t\n323\t// NewValidationError creates a new ValidationError with the given parameters.\n324\t//\n325\t// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n326\t// ValidationError instances with message, path, constraint, and error code.\n327\t//\n328\t// Parameters:\n329\t//   - filePath: Path to the file being validated\n330\t//   - message: Human-readable error message\n331\t//   - fieldPath: Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n332\t//   - constraint: Constraint that was violated (optional)\n333\t//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n334\t//\n335\t// Returns a properly initialized ValidationError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n336\t//\n337\t// Example usage:\n338\t//\n339\t//\terr := NewValidationError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid port number\", \"server.port\", \"must be between 1-65535\", ErrCodeInvalidValue)\n340\tfunc NewValidationError(filePath string, message string, fieldPath string, constraint string, code ErrorCode) *ValidationError {\n341\t\t// Use provided code or default to generic validation error\n342\t\terrorCode := code\n343\t\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n344\t\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeValidationFailed\n345\t\t}\n346\t\n347\t\treturn &ValidationError{\n348\t\t\tFilePath:   filePath,\n349\t\t\tMessage:    message,\n350\t\t\tFieldPath:  fieldPath,\n351\t\t\tConstraint: constraint,\n352\t\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n353\t\t\tType:       ErrorTypeValidation,\n354\t\t}\n355\t}\n356\t\n357\t// IsValidationError checks if an error is a ValidationError.\n358\tfunc IsValidationError(err error) bool {\n359\t\tvar ve *ValidationError\n360\t\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &ve) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeValidation))\n361\t}\n362\t\n363\t// containsRequiredFieldKeywords checks if the message suggests a required field error.\n364\tfunc containsRequiredFieldKeywords(msg string) bool {\n365\t\tkeywords := []string{\"required\", \"missing\", \"not found\", \"must be provided\"}\n366\t\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n367\t\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n368\t\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n369\t\t\t\treturn true\n370\t\t\t}\n371\t\t}\n372\t\treturn false\n373\t}\n374\t\n375\t// containsConstraintKeywords checks if the message suggests a constraint violation.\n376\tfunc containsConstraintKeywords(msg string) bool {\n377\t\tkeywords := []string{\"constraint\", \"range\", \"length\", \"pattern\", \"invalid value\"}\n378\t\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n379\t\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n380\t\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n381\t\t\t\treturn true\n382\t\t\t}\n383\t\t}\n384\t\treturn false\n385\t}\n386\t\n387\t// FileError represents errors that occur during file I/O operations.\n388\t//\n389\t// FileError wraps file I/O errors and provides detailed\n390\t// information about the file operation that failed.\n391\ttype FileError struct {\n392\t\tOp        string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Op\" for backward compatibility\n393\t\tPath      string    // Path to the file\n394\t\tOperation string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Operation\" for clarity\n395\t\tMessage   string    // Human-readable error message\n396\t\tErr       error     // Underlying error for error wrapping\n397\t\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n398\t}\n399\t\n400\t// Code implements YAMLError interface.\n401\tfunc (fe *FileError) Code() ErrorCode {\n402\t\tif fe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n403\t\t\treturn fe.ErrorCode\n404\t\t}\n405\t\treturn ErrCodeFileIOError\n406\t}\n407\t\n408\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n409\tfunc (fe *FileError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n410\t\treturn ErrorTypeFile\n411\t}\n412\t\n413\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n414\tfunc (fe *FileError) Context() string {\n415\t\top := fe.Operation\n416\t\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n417\t\t\top = fe.Op\n418\t\t}\n419\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"operation: %s, path: %s\", op, fe.Path)\n420\t}\n421\t\n422\t// Error implements the error interface.\n423\tfunc (fe *FileError) Error() string {\n424\t\top := fe.Operation\n425\t\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n426\t\t\top = fe.Op\n427\t\t}\n428\t\tif op != \"\" {\n429\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"file error during %s on %s: %s\", op, fe.Path, fe.Message)\n430\t\t}\n431\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"file error in %s: %s\", fe.Path, fe.Message)\n432\t}\n433\t\n434\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\n435\tfunc (fe *FileError) Unwrap() error {\n436\t\treturn fe.Err\n437\t}\n438\t\n439\t// IsFileError checks if an error is a FileError.\n440\tfunc IsFileError(err error) bool {\n441\t\tvar fe *FileError\n442\t\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &fe) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeFile))\n443\t}\n444\t\n445\t// SyntaxError represents YAML syntax errors during parsing.\n446\t//\n447\t// SyntaxError includes indentation errors, malformed YAML, and other\n448\t// syntax-related issues that prevent the YAML from being parsed.\n449\ttype SyntaxError struct {\n450\t\tFilePath  string    // Path to the file with syntax error\n457\t\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n458\t}\n459\t\n460\t// Code implements YAMLError interface.\n461\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) Code() ErrorCode {\n462\t\tif se.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n463\t\t\treturn se.ErrorCode\n464\t\t}\n465\t\treturn ErrCodeInvalidSyntax\n466\t}\n467\t\n468\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n469\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n470\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n471\t}\n472\t\n473\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n474\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) Context() string {\n475\t\tctx := fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", se.Expected)\n476\t\tif se.Found != \"\" {\n477\t\t\tctx += fmt.Sprintf(\", found: %s\", se.Found)\n478\t\t}\n479\t\treturn ctx\n480\t}\n481\t\n482\t// Error implements the error interface.\n483\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) Error() string {\n484\t\tif se.Line > 0 {\n485\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"syntax error in %s at line %d, column %d: %s\", se.FilePath, se.Line, se.Column, se.Message)\n486\t\t}\n487\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"syntax error in %s: %s\", se.FilePath, se.Message)\n488\t}\n489\t\n490\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error.\n491\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) Unwrap() error {\n492\t\treturn se.Err\n493\t}\n494\t\n495\t// StructureError represents YAML structure errors during parsing.\n496\t//\n497\t// StructureError includes issues like duplicate keys, invalid nesting,\n498\t// and other structural problems that syntax-checking alone won't catch.\n499\ttype StructureError struct {\n500\t\tFilePath     string    // Path to the file ste.DuplicateKey)\n530\t\t}\n531\t\treturn ctx\n532\t}\n533\t\n534\t// Error implements the error interface.\n535\tfunc (ste *StructureError) Error() string {\n536\t\tif ste.Line > 0 {\n537\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"structure error in %s at line %d: %s\", ste.FilePath, ste.Line, ste.Message)\n538\t\t}\n539\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"structure error in %s: %s\", ste.FilePath, ste.Message)\n540\t}\n541\t\n542\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error.\n543\tfunc (ste *StructureError) Unwrap() error {\n544\t\treturn ste.Err\n545\t}\n546\t\n547\t// TypeMismatchError represents tme.Value)\n578\t}\n579\t\n580\t// Error implements the error interface.\n581\tfunc (tme *TypeMismatchError) Error() string {\n582\t\tif tme.Line > 0 {\n583\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"type mismatch in  // Path to the file with duplicate keys\n676\t\tKey       string    // The duplicate key name\n677\t\tLocation  string    // Nested path to the duplicate key\n678\t\tLine1     int       // Line %d, duplicate at line %d)\", dke.Line1, dke.Line2)\n704\t\t}\n705\t\treturn ctx\n706\t}\n707\t\n708\t// Error implements the error interface.\n709\tfunc (dke *DuplicateKeyError) Error() string {\n710\t\tif dke.Line2 > 0 {\n711\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"duplicate key error in %s at line %d: key %q already defined at line %d\",\n712\t\t\t\tdke.FilePath, dke.Line2, dke.Key, dke.Line1)\n713\t\t}\n714\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"duplicate key error in %s: key %q\", dke.FilePath, dke.Key)\n715\t}\n716\t\n717\t// SchemaLoadError represents errors loading schema YAMLError of the specified type.\n805\tfunc isYAMLErrorOfType(err error, errorType ErrorType) bool {\n806\t\tif err == nil {\n807\t\t\treturn false\n808\t\t}\n809\t\n810\t\t// Try to unwrap to get to errorType\n814\t\t\t}\n815\t\t}\n816\t\treturn false\n817\t}\n818\t\n819\t// IsYAMLError checks if an error is any type of YAMLError.\n820\tfunc IsYAMLError(err error) bool {\n821\t\tif err == nil Package yamlutil provides comprehensive error type definitions for YAML processing.\n//\n// Error Hierarchy:\n//\n//\tYAMLError (base interface)\n//\t├── FileError (file I/O errors)\n//\t├── ParseError (YAML parsing errors)\n//\t│   ├── SyntaxError (YAML syntax errors)\n//\t│   ├── StructureError (YAML structure errors)\n//\t│   └── TypeMismatchError (type conversion errors)\n//\t├── ValidationError (validation errors)\n//\t│   ├── FieldNotFoundError (missing required fields)\n//\t│   ├── ConstraintError (constraint violations)\n//\t│   └── DuplicateKeyError (duplicate key errors)\n//\t└── SchemaError (schema-related errors)\n//\t\t├── SchemaLoadError (schema loading errors)\n//\t\t└── SchemaValidationError (schema validation errors)\npackage yamlutil\n\nimport (\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// YAMLError is the base interface for all YAML processing errors.\n//\n// This interface provides a common type for all errors that occur during\n// YAML file I/O, parsing, validation, and schema operations.\ntype YAMLError interface {\n\terror\n\n\t// Code returns the error code for programmatic error handling.\n\tCode() ErrorCode\n\n\t// YAMLErrorType returns the category of error for type switching.\n\tYAMLErrorType() ErrorType\n\n\t// Context returns additional context about the error.\n\tContext() string\n}\n\n// ErrorType represents the category of YAML error.\ntype ErrorType string\n\nconst (\n\tErrorTypeFile           ErrorType = \"file\"           // File I/O errors\n\tErrorTypeParse          ErrorType = \"parse\"          // YAML parsing errors\n\tErrorTypeSyntax         ErrorType = \"syntax\"         // YAML syntax errors\n\tErrorTypeStructure      ErrorType = \"structure\"      // YAML structure errors\n\tErrorTypeTypeMismatch   ErrorType = \"type_mismatch\"  // Type conversion errors\n\tErrorTypeValidation     ErrorType = \"validation\"     // Validation errors\n\tErrorTypeFieldNotFound  ErrorType = \"field_not_found\" // Missing required fields\n\tErrorTypeConstraint     ErrorType = \"constraint\"     // Constraint violations\n\tErrorTypeDuplicateKey   ErrorType = \"duplicate_key\"  // Duplicate key errors\n\tErrorTypeSchema         ErrorType = \"schema\"         // Schema-related errors\n\tErrorTypeSchemaLoad     ErrorType = \"schema_load\"     // Schema loading errors\n\tErrorTypeSchemaValidate ErrorType = \"schema_validate\" // Schema validation errors\n\tErrorTypeUnknown        ErrorType = \"unknown\"        // Unknown error type\n\tErrorTypeEmpty          ErrorType = \"empty\"          // Empty content\n\tErrorTypeIO             ErrorType = \"io\"             // I/O error\n)\n\n// ErrorCode represents specific error codes for programmatic error handling.\n//\n// ErrorCodes provide machine-readable identifiers for errors, enabling\n// programmatic error handling and internationalization of error messages.\ntype ErrorCode string\n\nconst (\n\t// File error codes\n\tErrCodeFileNotFound      ErrorCode = \"FILE_NOT_FOUND\"      // File does not exist\n\tErrCodeFileAccessDenied ErrorCode = \"FILE_ACCESS_DENIED\" // Permission denied\n\tErrCodeFileIOError       ErrorCode = \"FILE_IO_ERROR\"      // Generic I/O error\n\tErrCodeFileEmpty         ErrorCode = \"FILE_EMPTY\"         // File is empty\n\n\t// Parse error codes\n\tErrCodeInvalidSyntax   ErrorCode = \"INVALID_SYNTAX\"    // YAML syntax error\n\tErrCodeTypeMismatch    ErrorCode = \"TYPE_MISMATCH\"    // Type conversion error\n\tErrCodeInvalidStructure ErrorCode = \"INVALID_STRUCTURE\" // YAML structure error\n\tErrCodeDuplicateKey    ErrorCode = \"DUPLICATE_KEY\"    // Duplicate mapping key\n\tErrCodeParseError       ErrorCode = \"PARSE_ERROR\"      // Generic parse error\n\n\t// Validation error codes\n\tErrCodeValidationFailed   ErrorCode = \"VALIDATION_FAILED\"    // Validation failed\n\tErrCodeRequiredField      ErrorCode = \"REQUIRED_FIELD\"       // Missing required field\n\tErrCodeConstraintViolation ErrorCode = \"CONSTRAINT_VIOLATION\" // Constraint violated\n\tErrCodeInvalidValue       ErrorCode = \"INVALID_VALUE\"        // Invalid value\n\n\t// Schema error codes\n\tErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed   ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_LOAD_FAILED\"    // Schema loading failed\n\tErrCodeSchemaValidation   ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_VALIDATION\"    // Schema validation failed\n\tErrCodeSchemaNotFound     ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_NOT_FOUND\"      // Schema not found\n\tErrCodeSchemaInvalid      ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_INVALID\"         // Invalid schema definition\n)\n\n// ParseError represents errors that occur during YAML parsing.\n//\n// ParseError wraps underlying parsing errors and provides detailed\n// location information (line, column) and context about what failed.\ntype ParseError struct {\n\tFilePath   string      // Path to the file being parsed\n\tLine       int         // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tColumn     int         // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tMessage    string      // Human-readable error message\n\tContextStr string      // Additional context about the parsing state\n\tErr        error       // Underlying error for error wrapping\n\tErrorType  ErrorType   // Specific type of parse error\n\tErrorCode  ErrorCode   // Error code for programmatic handling\n\tExpected   string      // What was expected (optional, for syntax/type errors)\n\tActual     string      // What was actually found (optional, for syntax/type errors)\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif pe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn pe.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeParseError\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn pe.ErrorType\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Context() string {\n\treturn pe.ContextStr\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Error() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\t// Build base error with location\n\tif pe.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s at line %d\", pe.FilePath, pe.Line))\n\t\tif pe.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", pe.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s\", pe.FilePath))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add message\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", pe.Message))\n\n\t// Add expected vs actual if available\n\tif pe.Expected != \"\" || pe.Actual != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", pe.Expected))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" && pe.Actual != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tif pe.Actual != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %s\", pe.Actual))\n\t\t}\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn pe.Err\n}\n\n// NewParseError creates a new ParseError with the given parameters.\n//\n// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n// ParseError instances with message, line, column, error code, and optional expected/actual values.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - filePath: Path to the file being parsed\n//   - message: Human-readable error message\n//   - (use empty string for default)\n//   - expected: What was expected (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n//   - actual: What was actually found (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n//\n// Returns a properly initialized ParseError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := NewParseError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid syntax\", 10, 5, ErrCodeInvalidSyntax, \"identifier\", \"123\")\nfunc NewParseError(filePath string, message string, line int, column int, code ErrorCode, expected string, actual string) *ParseError {\n\t// Use provided code or default to generic parse error\n\terrorCode := code\n\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeParseError\n\t}\n\n\treturn &ParseError{\n\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n\t\tMessage:   message,\n\t\tLine:      line,\n\t\tColumn:    column,\n\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n\t\tErrorType: ErrorTypeParse,\n\t\tExpected:  expected,\n\t\tActual:    actual,\n\t}\n}\n\n// IsParseError checks if an error is a ParseError.\nfunc IsParseError(err error) bool {\n\tvar pe *ParseError\n\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &pe) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeParse))\n}\n\n// ValidationError represents errors that occur during YAML validation.\n//\n// ValidationError wraps validation failures and provides detailed\n// information about what failed validation. This is the base error type\n// used throughout the yamlutil package for validation errors.\ntype ValidationError struct {\n\tFilePath   string    // Path to the file being validated\n\tFieldPath  string    // Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n\tMessage    string    // Human-readable error message\n\tLine       int       // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tColumn     int       // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, optional)\n\tConstraint string    // Constraint that was violated (optional)\n\tContextStr string    // Additional context about the validation state (optional)\n\tErr        error     // Underlying error for error wrapping (optional)\n\tErrorCode  ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling (optional)\n\tType       ErrorType // Category of error for type switching\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif ve.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ve.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeValidationFailed\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\t// If Type is explicitly set, use it\n\tif ve.Type != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ve.Type\n\t}\n\t// Try to determine error type from error code or message\n\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeRequiredField || (ve.FieldPath != \"\" && containsRequiredFieldKeywords(ve.Message)) {\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeFieldNotFound\n\t}\n\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeConstraintViolation || containsConstraintKeywords(ve.Message) {\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeConstraint\n\t}\n\treturn ErrorTypeValidation\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Context() string {\n\treturn ve.ContextStr\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Error() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\t// Build base error with location\n\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s at line %d\", ve.FilePath, ve.Line))\n\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", ve.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s\", ve.FilePath))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add field path if available\n\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" at field %s\", ve.FieldPath))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add message\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", ve.Message))\n\n\t// Add constraint if available\n\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (constraint: %s)\", ve.Constraint))\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn ve.Err\n}\n\n// String returns a formatted error message with context.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) String() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", ve.Message))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Type: %s\\n\", ve.YAMLErrorType()))\n\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Location: Line %d\", ve.Line))\n\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Column %d\", ve.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t}\n\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Field: %s\\n\", ve.FieldPath))\n\t}\n\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Constraint: %s\\n\", ve.Constraint))\n\t}\n\tif ve.ContextStr != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context: %s\\n\", ve.ContextStr))\n\t}\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// NewValidationError creates a new ValidationError with the given parameters.\n//\n// This constructor function provides a - message: Human-readable error message\n//   - fieldPath: Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n//   - constraint: Constraint that was violated (optional)\n//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n//\n// Returns a properly initialized ValidationError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := NewValidationError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid port number\", \"server.port\", \"must be between 1-65535\", ErrCodeInvalidValue)\nfunc NewValidationError(filePath string, message string, fieldPath ErrCodeValidationFailed\n\t}\n\n\treturn &ValidationError{\n\t\tFilePath:   filePath,\n\t\tMessage:    message,\n\t\tFieldPath:  fieldPath,\n\t\tConstraint: constraint,\n\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n\t\tType:       ErrorTypeValidation,\n\t}\n}\n\n// IsValidationError checks if an error is a ValidationError.\nfunc IsValidationError(err error) bool {\n\tvar ve *ValidationError\n\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &ve) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeValidation))\n}\n\n// containsRequiredFieldKeywords checks if the message suggests a required field error.\nfunc containsRequiredFieldKeywords(msg string) bool {\n\tkeywords := []string{\"required\", \"missing\", \"not found\", \"must be provided\"}\n\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// containsConstraintKeywords checks if the message suggests a constraint violation.\nfunc containsConstraintKeywords(msg string) bool {\n\tkeywords := []string{\"constraint\", \"range\", \"length\", \"pattern\", \"invalid value\"}\n\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// FileError represents errors that occur during file I/O operations.\n//\n// FileError wraps file I/O errors and provides detailed\n// information about the file operation that failed.\ntype FileError struct {\n\tOp        string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Op\" for backward compatibility\n\tPath      string    // Path to the file\n\tOperation string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Operation\" for clarity\n\tMessage   string    // Human-readable error message\n\tErr       error     // Underlying error for error wrapping\n\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (fe *FileError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif fe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fe.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeFileIOError\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (fe *FileError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeFile\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (fe *FileError) Context() string {\n\top := fe.Operation\n\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n\t\top = fe.Op\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"operation: %s, path: %s\", op, fe.Path)\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (fe *FileError) Error() string {\n\top := fe.Operation\n\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n\t\top = fe.Op\n\t}\n\tif op != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"file error during %s on %s: %s\", op, fe.Path, fe.Message)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"file error in %s: %s\", fe.Path, fe.Message)\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\nfunc (fe *FileError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn fe.Err\n}\n\n// parsed.\ntype SyntaxError struct {\n\tFilePath  string    // Path to the file with syntax error\n\tLine      int       // Line number where syntax error occurred\n\tColumn    int       // Column {\n\tif se.Line > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"syntax error in %s at line %d, column %d: %s\", se.FilePath, se.Line, se.Column, se.Message)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"syntax error in %s: %s\", {\n\tFilePath     string    // Path to the file with structure error\n\tLine         int       // Line number where structure error occurred\n\tMessage      string    // Description of the structure ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ste *StructureError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif ste.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ste.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\tif ste.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ErrCodeDuplicateKey\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeInvalidStructure\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ste *StructureError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeStructure\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ste *StructureError) Context() string {\n\tctx := \"location: \" + ste.Location\n\tif ste.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n\t\tctx += fmt.Sprintf(\", duplicate key: %s\", ste.DuplicateKey)\n\t}\n\treturn ctx\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (ste *StructureError) Error() string {\n\tif ste.Line > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"structure error in %s at line %d: %s\", ste.FilePath, ste.Line, ste.Message)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"structure error in %s: %s\", ste.FilePath, ste.Message)\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error.\nfunc (ste *StructureError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn ste.Err\n}\n\n// TypeMismatchError represents type conversion errors during parsing.\n//\n// TypeMismatchError occurs when YAML content cannot be converted to the\n// expected Go type (e.g., trying to parse a string as an integer).\ntype TypeMismatchError struct {\n\tFilePath     string    // Path to the file with type error\n\tFieldPath    string    // Dot-notation path to the field with error\n\tExpectedType string    // Expected type description\n\tActualType   string    // Actual type found\n\tValue        string    // Actual value that caused the error\n\tLine         int       // Line number where error occurred\n\tErrorCode    ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (tme *TypeMismatchError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif tme.ErrorCode != \"\" ErrorTypeTypeMismatch\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (tme *TypeMismatchError) Context() string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"field: %s, expected type: %s, actual type: %s, value: %s\",\n\t\ttme.FieldPath, tme.ExpectedType, tme.ActualType, tme.Value)\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (tme *TypeMismatchError) Error() string {\n\tif tme.Line > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"type mismatch in %s at line %d, field %s: expected %s, got %s\",\n\t\t\ttme.FilePath, tme.Line, tme.FieldPath, tme.ExpectedType, tme.ActualType)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"type mismatch in %s, field %s: expected %s, got %s\",\n\t\ttme.FilePath, tme.FieldPath, tme.ExpectedType, tme.ActualType)\n}\n\n// FieldNotFoundError represents an error when a required field is missing.\n//\n// FieldNotFoundError is used when accessing required fields that don't exist\n// in the YAML data structure.\ntype FieldNotFoundError struct {\n\tFilePath  string    // Path to the file missing the field\n\tFieldPath string    // Dot-notation path to the missing field\n\tLine      int       // Line number where field should be (if known)\n\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for error.\ntype DuplicateKeyError struct {\n\tFilePath  string    // Path to the file with duplicate keys\n\tKey       string    // The duplicate key name\n\tLocation  string    // Nested path to the handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (dke *DuplicateKeyError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif dke.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn dke.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn dke.Location)\n\t}\n\tif dke.Line1 > 0 && dke.Line2 > 0 {\n\t\tctx += fmt.Sprintf(\" (first at line %d, duplicate at line %d)\", dke.Line1, dke.Line2)\n\t}\n\treturn ctx\n}\n\n// Error implements the checking\n\n// isYAMLErrorOfType checks if an error is a YAMLError of the specified type.\nfunc isYAMLErrorOfType(err error, errorType ErrorType) bool {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Try to unwrap to get to the base YAMLError\n\tfor unwrapped := err; unwrapped != nil; unwrapped = errors.Unwrap(unwrapped) {\n\t\tif ye, ok := unwrapped.(YAMLError); ok if an error indicates a file was not found.\n// This works with both FileError and other wrapped file not found errors.\nfunc IsFileNotFoundError(err error) bool {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if error chain contains os.ErrNotExist\n\tif os.IsNotExist(err) {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\t// Check wrapped errors\n\tfor unwrapped := err; unwrapped != nil; unwrapped = Invalid indentation\n+\t// - Malformed YAML structure\n+\t// - Invalid escape sequences\n+\t// - Improper quoting\n+\t// - Invalid YAML tokens\n+\tParseErrorVariantSyntax ParseErrorVariant = \"syntax\"\n+\n+\t// ParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch represents type conversion errors.\n+\t//\n+\t// This variant covers errors when YAML content cannot be converted to the\n+\t// expected Go type, including:\n+\t// - Attempting to parse a string as an integer\n+\t// - Trying to unmarshal a scalar into a struct\n+\t// - Incompatible array/slice types\n+\t// - Invalid boolean representations\n+\tParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch ParseErrorVariant = \"type_mismatch\"\n+\n+\t// ParseErrorVariantValidation represents constraint validation errors.\n+\t//\n+\t// This variant covers errors when YAML data violates defined constraints,\n+\t// including:\n+\t// - Value out of allowed range\n+\t// - String length violations\n+\t// - Pattern matching failures\n+\t// - Required field violations\n+\tParseErrorVariantValidation ParseErrorVariant = \"validation\"\n+\n+\t// ParseErrorVariantIO represents I/O operation errors.\n+\t//\n+\t// This variant covers errors related to file operations during parsing,\n+\t// including:\n+\t// -
